title: Selective Progressive Test-Time Prompt Adaptation for Vision Transformers
description: SPT-TTA is a novel test-time adaptation framework for Vision Transformers that sequentially adapts visual prompts layer-by-layer during inference, with adaptive layer selection based on attention entropy. Unlike existing methods that adapt all prompts simultaneously (VPA, DePT), SPT-TTA processes adaptation as a cascade where each layer adapts before subsequent layers process features, preventing gradient conflicts and enabling progressive refinement.
motivation: Existing visual prompt adaptation methods (VPA, DePT) adapt all prompts simultaneously, ignoring that different transformer layers encode different levels of visual abstraction. Early layers capture low-level features while deeper layers encode high-level semantics. Sequential adaptation allows early layers to stabilize before deeper layers adapt, and adaptive layer selection controls computational cost by focusing on layers with high uncertainty (typically 40% of layers).
proposed_approach: SPT-TTA introduces three key innovations: (1) Sequential Layer-wise Adaptation: prompts are optimized sequentially during test-time, with each layer adapting before the next layer processes features—fundamentally different from simultaneous adaptation; (2) Adaptive Layer Selection: attention entropy-guided selection of which layers to adapt, reducing computational cost; (3) Unified Objective with Layer Weighting: a single adaptation objective combining entropy minimization and feature consistency, with theoretically-grounded layer-specific weights (λ_l = 0.3 for early, 0.5 for middle, 0.7 for deep layers) reflecting increasing importance of discriminative adaptation versus feature preservation.

related_work.paper: VPA: Fully Test-Time Visual Prompt Adaptation
related_work.authors: Jiachen Sun, Mark Ibrahim, Melissa Hall, Ivan Evtimov, Z. Morley Mao, Cristian Canton-Ferrer, Caner Hazirbas
related_work.contribution: First framework generalizing visual prompting with test-time adaptation using simultaneous optimization of all prompts
related_work.difference: VPA adapts all prompts simultaneously; SPT-TTA adapts sequentially layer-by-layer

related_work.paper: Visual Prompt Tuning for Test-Time Domain Adaptation (DePT)
related_work.contribution: Data-efficient prompt tuning with stage-wise prompting; DePT-Deep uses per-layer prompts adapted simultaneously
related_work.difference: DePT adapts prompts simultaneously at test-time; SPT-TTA uses sequential cascade adaptation

related_work.paper: TENT: Fully Test-Time Adaptation by Entropy Minimization
related_work.authors: Dequan Wang, Evan Shelhamer, Shaoteng Liu, Bruno Olshausen, Trevor Darrell
related_work.contribution: Early TTA method updating BatchNorm statistics via entropy minimization
related_work.difference: TENT updates normalization layers; SPT-TTA adapts visual prompts sequentially

related_work.paper: MEMO: Test Time Robustness via Adaptation and Augmentation
related_work.authors: Marvin Zhang, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn
related_work.contribution: Single-instance entropy minimization for test-time adaptation
related_work.difference: MEMO focuses on augmentation-based adaptation; SPT-TTA uses sequential layer-wise adaptation

related_work.paper: TDA: Training-free Dynamic Adapter for Test-Time Adaptation
related_work.authors: Adilbek Karmanov, Dayan Guan, Shijian Lu, Abdulmotaleb El Saddik, Eric Xing
related_work.contribution: Training-free adapter with key-value cache for progressive pseudo-label refinement
related_work.difference: TDA is training-free with cache mechanism; SPT-TTA uses gradient-based sequential adaptation

related_work.contribution: Introduces learnable tokens to ViT inputs while freezing backbone parameters
related_work.difference: VPT is a training-time method; SPT-TTA extends to test-time sequential adaptation

related_work.paper: Stabilizing Transformer Training by Preventing Attention Entropy Collapse
related_work.contribution: Theoretical connection between attention entropy and model stability
related_work.difference: Used for training stability; SPT-TTA leverages entropy for test-time layer selection

hypothesis: By selectively adapting prompts layer-by-layer during test-time, where each layer adapts before subsequent layers process features, we can achieve more effective distribution shift mitigation compared to simultaneous adaptation. Specifically: (1) Sequential adaptation will show comparable or better accuracy than simultaneous adaptation with lower variance across corruption types; (2) Attention entropy-based layer selection will reduce latency to 3-5× standard inference while retaining >85% of full sequential adaptation accuracy.

confirmatory: Achieve ≥72% accuracy on ImageNet-C (severity 3) with ViT-B/16, competitive with VPA (~70-71%) and DePT (~72-73%)
Show ≥5% improvement over source model (comparable to VPA's 6.5% corruption robustness improvement)
Sequential adaptation shows comparable or lower variance across corruption types vs. simultaneous adaptation
Adaptive layer selection retains >85% of full sequential adaptation accuracy
refutatory: Simultaneous adaptation exceeds sequential adaptation by >1%
Adaptive layer selection retains <75% of full sequential adaptation accuracy
Adaptation time exceeds 8× standard inference latency

novelty_claims: First method to perform sequential, layer-by-layer prompt adaptation at test time (vs. simultaneous adaptation in VPA/DePT)
Novel application of attention entropy for layer selection (vs. sample selection in U-VPA)
Unified objective with theoretically-grounded layer-specific weighting based on feature abstraction hierarchy

computational_requirements.primary_benchmarks: ImageNet-C (15 corruption types, 5 severity levels), ImageNet-V2
